---
title: "panel"
output: html_document
date: "2026-03-11"
---

Chargement des packages nécessaires
```{r}
library(ggplot2)   
library(dplyr)    
library(tidyr)     
library(readr)     
set.seed(123)
```

Définition des paramètres du panel
```{r}
n_eleves <- 1000
annees <- c("CP", "CE1", "CE2", "CM1")
```

Définition des variables
```{r}
eleves <- tibble(
  id_eleve = 1:n_eleves,
  taille_famille = sample(2:6, n_eleves, replace = TRUE),
  monoparental = rbinom(n_eleves, 1, 0.2),
  pcs_parent = sample(c("cadre","profession_intermediaire","employe","ouvrier"),
                      n_eleves, replace = TRUE, prob = c(0.25,0.25,0.30,0.20)),
  diplome_parent = sample(c("brevet","bac","bac+3","bac+5"), n_eleves, replace = TRUE),
  revenu_mensuel = round(rnorm(n_eleves, 2500, 800)),
  logement = sample(c("maison","appartement","logement_social"),
                    n_eleves, replace = TRUE, prob = c(0.45,0.35,0.20)),
  origine_migratoire = sample(c("aucune","un_parent","deux_parents"),
                              n_eleves, replace = TRUE, prob = c(0.75,0.15,0.10)),
  aspiration_famille = sample(c("CAP","BAC","BAC+3","BAC+5"), n_eleves, replace = TRUE)
)
```

Génération des données aléatoires du panel
```{r}
panel <- expand_grid(
  annee = annees,
  id_eleve = eleves$id_eleve  # inversé : année d'abord
) %>%
  left_join(eleves, by = "id_eleve") %>%
  # Rapport à l'école
  mutate(
    implication_parents = sample(1:5, n(), replace = TRUE),
    opinion_ecole = sample(1:5, n(), replace = TRUE)
  ) %>%
  group_by(id_eleve) %>%
  mutate(
    progression_math = cumsum(rnorm(n(), mean = 5, sd = 5)),
    progression_fr = cumsum(rnorm(n(), mean = 4, sd = 4)),
    score_math = 200 + progression_math + rnorm(n(),0,10) +
      ifelse(pcs_parent=="cadre",15,0) - ifelse(pcs_parent=="ouvrier",15,0),
    score_fr = 210 + progression_fr + rnorm(n(),0,10) +
      ifelse(pcs_parent=="cadre",10,0) - ifelse(pcs_parent=="ouvrier",10,0),
    redoublement = rbinom(n(), 1, 0.1),
    orientation = case_when(
      annee %in% c("CP","CE1") ~ "general",
      annee == "CE2" ~ sample(c("general","technologique","professionnel"), 1,
                              prob=c(0.6,0.25,0.15)),
      annee == "CM1" ~ sample(c("general","technologique","professionnel"), 1,
                              prob=c(0.55,0.25,0.20))
    ),
    aspiration_eleve = case_when(
      score_math + score_fr > 520 ~ "BAC+5",
      score_math + score_fr > 480 ~ "BAC+3",
      score_math + score_fr > 440 ~ "BAC",
      TRUE ~ "CAP"
    )
  ) %>%
  ungroup()
```

Simulation de l'attrition
```{r}
panel <- panel %>%
  mutate(
    proba_sortie = case_when(
      annee == "CP" ~ 0,
      annee == "CE1" ~ 0.05,
      annee == "CE2" ~ 0.08,
      annee == "CM1" ~ 0.10
    ),
    sortie = rbinom(n(), 1, proba_sortie)
  ) %>%
  group_by(id_eleve) %>%
  mutate(cumul_sortie = cumsum(sortie)) %>%
  filter(cumul_sortie == 0) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  select(-proba_sortie, -sortie, -cumul_sortie, -progression_math, -progression_fr)
```

Vérification de la taille du panel
```{r}
panel %>% count(annee)
```

Sauvegarder les données sous un fichier CSV
```{r}
library(readr)
write_csv(panel, "panel_eleves_longitudinal_attrition.csv")
```
